sources.label: Taipei Times (Jun 4, 2026): Aspeed expects revenue to increase, citing agentic-AI server demand; substrate (T-glass to E-glass) bottleneck pushes some shipments to 2027, easing in Q4
BigGo Finance (Jun 1, 2026): Q1 earnings call - record EPS NT$37.4, Q3 revenue guidance NT$4.1-4.3bn capped by supply bottlenecks, chairman sees 2027 growth 'beyond imagination'

verdicts.rankRationale: No genuinely new information since June 6 - the bullish earnings call (Jun 1) and chairman commentary (Jun 4) predate the cutoff and were already reflected in yesterday's 'down' verdict on rerating grounds. Today's +1.9% grind to +146% YTD without fresh evidence is drift, not a setup change, so hold at rank 23 with mild negative drift for continued crowding.
verdicts.bull: ASPEED is a near-monopoly (~70% share) in server BMCs with the premium-ASP AST2700 ramping on every AI and general-purpose server; management guides H2 > H1 and says 2027 growth will be 'beyond imagination', with a 41% raised 2030 BMC TAM.
verdicts.neutral: The story is real but fully told: Taiwan's highest-priced stock, 15 buy ratings and zero sells, +146% YTD, USD 21bn cap - the bottleneck is recognized, so residual alpha at rank 23 is roughly fair for this page's selective-exposure tier.
verdicts.bear: Substrate shortages cap Q3 revenue (NT$4.1-4.3bn) and push shipments into 2027 while gross margin slipped below the 70% target; at this multiple any AI-server order digestion or capex pause triggers a sharp de-rating of a very crowded name.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May monthly revenue release (due ~June 10) and Q4 confirmation that the T-glass/E-glass substrate bottleneck is resolved, unlocking deferred shipments.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating positioning: a hyperscaler capex pause or slower AST2700 ramp compresses the premium multiple far faster than fundamentals deteriorate.
